moves.name: Stand LP
moves.notes: Chains into stand LP / crouch LP / crouch LK | Has several Target Combo follow-ups via the classic Bushin Chain

moves.name: Stand MP
moves.notes: Has a MP > HP Target Combo follow-up (Bread and Butter combo tool)

moves.name: Stand HP
moves.notes: Strong poke and shimmy tool that's hit confirmable (17F hit confirm window) | Incredibly strong whiff punish tool with its forward movement and big staggering state on Punish Counter (+23) which allows Kimberly to combo with whatever she wants

moves.name: Stand LK
moves.notes: Pretty good range for a fully cancelable light | Incurs 20% damage scaling penalty if used as the first hit of a combo

moves.name: Stand MK
moves.notes: Good footsie / buffer tool that's fully cancelable | Incurs 15% damage scaling penalty if used as the first hit of a combo

moves.name: Stand HK
moves.notes: Upper body invincible on airborne strikes on frames 10-15 | Great combo and frametrap tool as well as a decent anti-air (although a bit slow start-up)

moves.name: Crouch LP
moves.notes: Chains into stand LP / crouch LP / crouch LK | Incurs 20% damage scaling penalty if used as the first hit of a combo

moves.name: Crouch MP
moves.notes: Primary combo / buffer tool | +2F extra recovery frames (15F) on whiff with an extended hurtbox until the end of recovery

moves.name: Crouch HP
moves.notes: Forces stand on hit | Decent anti-air

moves.name: Crouch LK
moves.notes: Chains into stand LP / crouch LP / crouch LK | Incurs 20% damage scaling penalty if used as the first hit of a combo

moves.name: Crouch MK
moves.notes: Great frame advantage on hit and block for a crouch MK that enables all sorts of combo and pressure opportunities | Incurs 20% damage scaling penalty if used as the first hit of a combo

moves.name: Crouch HK
moves.notes: Has very strong hitbox priority | Has some Juggle potential naturally without Drive Rush

moves.name: Jump LP
moves.notes: Quick air-to-air check

moves.name: Jump MP
moves.notes: Puts airborne opponents into a limited juggle state | Cancelable into airborne specials and Level 2 Super

moves.name: Jump HP
moves.notes: Causes a spiked knockdown on airborne opponents | Projectile invincible on lower body (elbow) on frames 8-30

moves.name: Jump LK
moves.notes: Cross-up | Can be used as a fuzzy instant Overhead after a deep jump-in Heavy (or jump-in Mediums in Burnout as well)

moves.name: Jump MK
moves.notes: Cross-up | Projectile invincible on lower body (back leg, not the kicking leg) on frames 7-30

moves.name: Jump HK
moves.notes: Solid horizontal jump-in tool and can be used to air reset opponents after f+HK (leaping kick) juggles for a mix-up | Projectile invincible on lower body (but not on the extended legs during the kick) on frames 9-35

moves.name: Elbow Drop
moves.notes: Not an Overhead | Halts Kimberly's air momentum and drops straight down

moves.name: Water Slicer Slide
moves.notes: Incredibly strong tool to bypass many projectiles even on reaction and getting combo conversions on Punish Counter | Low profile and projectile invincible on upper body on frames 9-21

moves.name: Windmill Kick
moves.notes: Overhead | Forces crouch on hit

moves.name: Hisen Kick
moves.notes: Airborne on frames 18-32 (Forced Knockdown state) | Kimberly can leap over low hitting attacks during the jump arc

moves.name: Step Up (neutral hop)
moves.notes: Forced Knockdown state while airborne | Start-up refers to how early Kimberly can do airborne follow-ups after the hop

moves.name: Step Up (forward hop)
moves.notes: Forced Knockdown state while airborne | Start-up refers to how early Kimberly can do airborne follow-ups after the hop

moves.name: Step Up (backward hop)
moves.notes: Forced Knockdown state while airborne | Start-up refers to how early Kimberly can do airborne follow-ups after the hop

moves.name: Bushin Prism Strikes 2
moves.notes: Launches opponent into a limited juggle state (can juggle afterwards with Tatsus) and has very high KDA for some oki setups (such as spray cans)

moves.name: Bushin Prism Strikes 3
moves.notes: Last hit (HK) spikes down opponent into a short OTG bounce state | +5F extra recovery frames (24F) on block only

moves.name: Bushin Hellchain 1
moves.notes: Keeps the opponent grounded and leaves Kimberly up close and +2 on Hit (can be good to maintain grounded pressure / mixups) | Can be a good tool against Drive Impact since the third hit will break armor and be +4 on Counter-Hit for a full combo conversion

moves.name: Bushin Hellchain Throw
moves.notes: Throws opponent behind Kimberly (switches sides)

moves.name: Bushin Tiger Fangs
moves.notes: Launches opponent into a limited juggle state

moves.name: Ripcord Throw
moves.notes: Throw Loop: Walk forward manually timed mixup between throw/strike (not possible on mid-screen back-roll) | Gets +70% damage increase (1838) damage), HKD (denies back-roll option on wake-up), and depletes 1 bar of Drive Gauge on a Punish Counter

moves.name: Bell Ringer
moves.notes: Back Throw oki: Basic meaties but no meaty throw | Gets +70% damage increase (1838 damage), HKD (denies back-roll option on wake-up), and depletes 1 bar of Drive Gauge on a Punish Counter

moves.name: Drive Impact: Steadfast Strike
moves.notes: 2 hits of armor on frames 1-27 | Damages opponents Drive Gauge on Block (1/2 bar) | Hit (1 bar) | Counter-Hit (1.25 bar) | Punish Counter (1.5 bar)

moves.name: Drive Reversal: Shapeless State
moves.notes: Fully invincible on frames 1-22 (frames 1-20 when used on wake-up as a Reversal) | Armor Break (blows through all armor like Drive Impact)

moves.name: Drive Parry
moves.notes: Parries all strikes and projectiles (high and low) from frame 1 | 12F parry window (can hold the parry to extend the parry window)

moves.name: Drive Rush
moves.notes: Dashes forward and enhances the next normal used out of the Drive Rush with +4 extra hitstun / blockstun (some moves also get unique properties enhanced such as juggle state) | The +4 bonus frame advantage gained from a Drive Rush enhanced move also stacks with any bonus frame advantage that move may have gained from Counter-Hit or Punish Counter

moves.name: LK Bushin Senpukyaku
moves.notes: Invincible to airborne attacks on frames 5-11 | Throw invincible on frames 7-11

moves.name: MK Bushin Senpukyaku
moves.notes: Invincible to airborne attacks on frames 6-12 | Throw invincible on frames 8-12

moves.name: HK Bushin Senpukyaku
moves.notes: Invincible to airborne attacks on frames 7-13 | Throw invincible on frames 9-13

moves.name: OD Bushin Senpukyaku
moves.notes: Invincible to airborne attacks on frames 1-11 | Throw invincible on frames 7-11

moves.name: Aerial Bushin Senpukyaku
moves.notes: Only possible during a forward jump | Can hit crouching if performed very late into the jump arc just before landing (can grant better frame advantage than doing it via the Tiger Knee motion)

moves.name: OD Aerial Bushin Senpukyaku
moves.notes: Only possible during a forward jump | Can hit crouching if performed very late into the jump arc just before landing (can grant better frame advantage than doing it via the Tiger Knee motion)

moves.name: LP Vagabond Edge
moves.notes: Last active frame has quite a bit more range than the first two, making this easier to space out to hit on last active frame (this makes it safe on block and can give a combo extension) | The last active frame causes a unique knockback animation on hit, making it easier to confirm

moves.name: MP Vagabond Edge
moves.notes: Second hit only comes out on hit | Only first hit is cancelable into Level 3 Super

moves.name: HP Vagabond Edge
moves.notes: Second hit only comes out on hit | Jump cancelable on HIT only by holding up (Kimberly always jumps forward)

moves.name: OD Vagabond Edge
moves.notes: Second hit only comes out on hit | Jump cancelable on HIT only by holding up (Kimberly always jumps forward)

moves.name: Sprint
moves.notes: Cancels into follow-ups after 8 frames minimum | You can hold in the button of the follow-up and forward direction if you want to do after inputting the Run (very helpful!)

moves.name: OD Sprint
moves.notes: Cancels into follow-ups after 8 frames minimum (can cancel into OD Stop 2F earlier) | You can hold in the button of the follow-up and forward direction if you want to do after inputting the Run (very helpful!)

moves.name: Sprint > Torso Cleaver
moves.notes: Extended legs are invincible to airborne strikes on frames 18-27 (not including the initial 8F of Run start-up) | Cannot hit cross-up (behind Kimberly)

moves.name: Sprint > Shadow Slide
moves.notes: Low profile and Projectile Invincible on her upper body on frames 6-21 (not including the initial 8F of Run start-up) | Low hitting slide that puts opponent into a limited juggle state

moves.name: Sprint > Neck Hunter
moves.notes: Overhead | Cancelable into Level 3 Super on Hit and Block (doesn't actually combo except in very high juggle scenarios)

moves.name: Sprint > Emergency Stop
moves.notes: Fastest possible Run > Stop is 22 frames total (8+14) | Latest possible Run > Stop is 55 frames total (41+14) which happens automatically if Kimberly doesn't collide with the opponent after running full-screen

moves.name: Sprint > Arc Step
moves.notes: Can only hit grounded opponents | Airborne on frames 22-56F (4F grounded recovery frames)

moves.name: Arc Step > Bushin Hojin Kick
moves.notes: Not an Overhead | Forced Knockdown state while airborne

moves.name: Arc Step > Bushin Izuna Otoshi
moves.notes: Grabs standing and crouch | Cannot grab airborne opponents

moves.name: OD Sprint > Torso Cleaver
moves.notes: Extended legs are invincible to airborne strikes on frames 14-23 (not including the initial 8F of Run start-up) | Cannot hit cross-up (behind Kimberly)

moves.name: OD Sprint > Shadow Slide
moves.notes: Projectile Invincible (except on the extended leg) on frames 4-19 (not including the 8F of Run start-up) | Low hitting slide that puts opponent into a limited juggle state

moves.name: OD Sprint > Neck Hunter
moves.notes: Overhead | Puts airborne opponents into an OTG juggle state (only Level 2/3 Super cancels seem to combo

moves.name: OD Sprint > Emergency Stop
moves.notes: Fastest possible Run > Stop is 19 frames total (6+13) | Latest possible Run > Stop is 54 frames total (41+13) which happens automatically if Kimberly doesn't collide with the opponent after running full-screen

moves.name: OD Sprint > Arc Step
moves.notes: Can only hit grounded opponents | Airborne on frames 20-54F (4F grounded recovery frames)

moves.name: OD Arc Step > Bushin Hojin Kick
moves.notes: Not an Overhead | Knockdown that also causes a wall splat when opponent is close enough to the wall (+43 KDA normally but +86 on a Wall Splat)

moves.name: OD Arc Step > Bushin Izuna Otoshi
moves.notes: Grabs standing and crouch | Cannot grab airborne opponents

moves.name: Hidden Variable
moves.notes: Fully invincible during on frames 19-26 (while Kimberly is off-screen) | Kimberly can cancel into any move she wants (except another Teleport or Drive Parry / Blocking) 7F (frame 34F total) after she re-appears (she cannot cancel into movement related options such as walking / dashing / jumping)

moves.name: OD Hidden Variable
moves.notes: Fully invincible during on frames 19-26 (while Kimberly is off-screen) | Airborne on frames 22-42 (Forced Knockdown state)

moves.name: Genius at Play
moves.notes: Refills 1 stock of Spray Can | Kimberly cannot re-stock on her Spray Cans until she has depleted her current stock

moves.name: OD Genius at Play
moves.notes: Refills 2 stocks of Spray Cans | Kimberly cannot re-stock on her Spray Cans until she has depleted her current stock

moves.name: Shuriken Bomb
moves.notes: Kimberly places a Spray Can on frame 18 that explodes after 122F (LP/L) | 124F (MP/M) | 128F (HP/H) total frames | Strength of the button dictates where Kimberly places her explosive Spray Can (Close: LP (Classic) / L (Modern) | Mid-screen: MP (Classic) / M (Modern) | Full-screen: HP (Classic) / H (Modern))

moves.name: Shuriken Bomb Spread
moves.notes: Kimberly places two different Spray Cans on frame 18 whose overall explosion start-up depends on their button strength; 122F (LP) | 124F (MP) | 128F (HP) total frames | Strength of the punch button (LP/MP/HP) dictates where Kimberly places her explosive Spray Can (Close: LP | Mid-screen: MP | Full-screen: HP)

moves.name: Nue Twister
moves.notes: Strike Throw (can be combo'd into) | Can be input via the Tiger Knee motion to perform it instantly off the ground

moves.name: OD Nue Twister
moves.notes: Strike Throw (can be combo'd into) | Incurs 20% damage scaling penalty if used as the first hit of a combo

moves.name: Bushin Beats (0 stock)
moves.notes: Strike and Throw invincible on frames 1-12 | Armor Break

moves.name: Bushin Beats (1 stock)
moves.notes: Gains +400 bonus damage and higher KDA (better oki) than regular version | Strike and Throw invincible on frames 1-12

moves.name: Bushin Scramble
moves.notes: Fully invincible on frame 1 only (during screen freeze) | Airborne on frames 2-20F (Forced Knockdown state)

moves.name: Soaring Bushin Scramble (air)
moves.notes: Fully invincible on frame 1 only (during screen freeze) | Forced Knockdown state while airborne

moves.name: Bushin Ninjastar Cypher
moves.notes: Fully invincible on frames 1-13 | Grants Kimberly +11% walk speed and damage bonus for the rest of the match

moves.name: Bushin Ninjastar Cypher (Critical Art)
moves.notes: Fully invincible on frames 1-13 | Grants Kimberly +11% walk speed and damage bonus for the rest of the match
